title: Learn English Chunks
prompt: Practice using English chunks to speak more naturally without translating word by word.
hint: Focus on memorizing whole phrases (chunks) instead of individual words to improve fluency.
sample: I was wondering if you could help me.

ja.prompt: 単語ごとに翻訳せず、英語のチャンク（まとまり）を使って自然に話す練習をしましょう。
ja.hint: 単語単位ではなく、フレーズ全体（チャンク）を覚えることに集中して流暢さを向上させましょう。
ja.sample: I was wondering if you could help me.

zh.prompt: 練習使用英語片語（chunks），讓你說話更自然，不用逐字翻譯。
zh.hint: 專注記憶整句片語，而非單字，提升流利度。
zh.sample: I was wondering if you could help me.

title: Use Polite Requests
prompt: Use the chunk 'I was wondering if' to politely ask for favors.
hint: Start your polite requests with 'I was wondering if' followed by your question.
sample: I was wondering if you could help me with my homework.

ja.prompt: 'I was wondering if' というチャンクを使って丁寧にお願いをしましょう。
ja.hint: 'I was wondering if' で始めて質問を続けてください。
ja.sample: I was wondering if you could help me with my homework.

zh.prompt: 使用片語 'I was wondering if' 禮貌地請求幫忙。
zh.hint: 用 'I was wondering if' 開頭，接著你的請求。
zh.sample: I was wondering if you could help me with my homework.

title: Summarize Stories Quickly
prompt: Use the chunk 'to make a long story short' to quickly summarize a story.
hint: Say 'to make a long story short' before giving the main point or conclusion.
sample: To make a long story short, we missed the train and had to wait an hour.

ja.prompt: 話を手短にまとめるために 'to make a long story short' を使いましょう。
ja.hint: 話の要点や結論を言う前に 'to make a long story short' と言います。
ja.sample: To make a long story short, we missed the train and had to wait an hour.

zh.prompt: 使用片語 'to make a long story short' 快速總結故事。
zh.hint: 在說重點或結論前說 'to make a long story short'。
zh.sample: To make a long story short, we missed the train and had to wait an hour.

title: Express Excitement for Future
prompt: Use the chunk 'I'm looking forward to' to express excitement about future events.
hint: Follow 'I'm looking forward to' with the event or activity you are excited about.
sample: I'm looking forward to the weekend.

ja.prompt: 'I'm looking forward to' を使って未来の出来事への期待を表現しましょう。
ja.hint: 'I'm looking forward to' の後に楽しみにしていることを続けます。
ja.sample: I'm looking forward to the weekend.

zh.prompt: 使用片語 'I'm looking forward to' 表達對未來事情的期待。
zh.hint: 'I'm looking forward to' 後面接你期待的活動或事件。
zh.sample: I'm looking forward to the weekend.

title: Say Things Are Unrelated
prompt: Use the chunk 'It has nothing to do with' to say two things are not connected.
hint: Use 'It has nothing to do with' followed by the subject that is unrelated.
sample: It has nothing to do with you.

ja.prompt: 'It has nothing to do with' を使って、二つのことが関係ないと言いましょう。
ja.hint: 'It has nothing to do with' の後に関係ない対象を続けます。
ja.sample: It has nothing to do with you.

zh.prompt: 使用片語 'It has nothing to do with' 表示兩件事無關。
zh.hint: 'It has nothing to do with' 後面接不相關的主題。
zh.sample: It has nothing to do with you.

title: Change the Topic Smoothly
prompt: Use the chunk 'By the way' to smoothly change the topic in conversation.
hint: Start a new topic with 'By the way' to shift the conversation naturally.
sample: By the way, how is your family?

ja.prompt: 会話の話題を変えるときに 'By the way' を使いましょう。
ja.hint: 自然に話題を変えたいときに 'By the way' で始めます。
ja.sample: By the way, how is your family?

zh.prompt: 使用片語 'By the way' 自然地轉換話題。
zh.hint: 想換話題時用 'By the way' 開頭。
zh.sample: By the way, how is your family?
